raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The Social Media Transparency and Accountability Act of 2021 requires social media platforms to publicly disclose their corporate policies regarding online hate, disinformation, extremism, harassment, and foreign interference.",
    "Investigations have shown that social media posts abetted and encouraged violent riots at the Capitol in January 2021.",
    "Efforts by social media companies to self-police online hate and disinformation have been widely criticized as grossly inadequate.",
    "The legislation requires social media platforms to file semi-annual reports on their corporate policies and enforcement efforts related to hate speech and disinformation.",
    "The bill mandates quarterly reports detailing key metrics regarding content flagged for violation, including the number of pieces of content and the methods of flagging utilized.",
    "The reporting requirements of the legislation aim to increase accountability for social media companies regarding the hate and harassment on their platforms.",
    "The legislation may provide evidence for legal action against social media companies if they do not make real progress in improving their policies."
  ]
}
```
